info.title: BASELINE Field Operations Intelligence API
info.description:
BASELINE captures operational truth through field visits and derives intelligence from that truth.
Every endpoint documents its required role, request/response shapes, and applicable audit log action.

**Role hierarchy**: Admin > Lead > UL (Unit Leader / field worker)
**Auth**: Bearer token (Azure Entra JWT) on all non-public endpoints.
**Labor safety**: No endpoint exposes per-worker performance metrics or identifiers
to Lead/UL roles. `captured_by_oid` is encrypted and Admin-access-only.

tags.name: Tenant
tags.description: Tenant onboarding: asset types, observation types, asset seeding (Admin)

AzureAD.description: Azure Active Directory (Entra) OAuth2. Pass the resulting access token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

BadRequest.description: Request validation failed — missing or invalid field

Unauthorized.description: Authentication required or Bearer token is invalid/expired

Forbidden.description: Caller lacks the required role for this endpoint

Conflict.description: State conflict — e.g. stop already completed or skipped

PayloadTooLarge.description: File exceeds the maximum allowed upload size (25 MB)

get.description: Public liveness probe. Returns 200 when the backend process is running.

get.summary: Identity probe — returns backend-validated token claims
get.description: Validates the Bearer token and returns the caller's sub, name, and roles.

get.summary: Authenticated ping — returns token payload and roles
get.description: Validates the Bearer token and echoes back the decoded payload. Useful for debugging token claims.
Returns a confirmation that the caller has the Admin role. Used for RBAC smoke testing.
Returns 200 if the caller has Admin role. Used as an RBAC test fixture.
Returns a confirmation that the caller has the UL role. Reserved for future inbox features.

get.summary: Get today's active route run for the authenticated UL
get.description: Looks up the most recent planned or in_progress route run assigned to the caller's Azure Entra OID. Returns the full route run with stop details. Also accessible to Lead and Admin for supervisory views.

404.description: No active assigned route run found

example.error: No active assigned route run found

post.summary: Upload photos for a route run stop
post.description: Accepts up to 10 image files via multipart/form-data. Each file is validated for MIME type (JPEG, PNG, WebP, HEIC) and size (≤25 MB). Files are stored in S3 and references saved to the database.

get.summary: List photos for a route run stop
get.description: Returns all photos attached to the specified route run stop.
Returns confirmation that the caller has the Lead role.

get.summary: Get all planned and in-progress route runs for today
get.description: Returns all active route runs across all pools. Used by the Lead dispatch view.
Returns the full route run including all stops. Lead and Admin only.

post.summary: Plan an OSRM-optimized route from a list of stop IDs
post.description: Takes an array of stop IDs, looks up their coordinates, and returns an OSRM-optimized trip order. Does not create a route run. Note: this endpoint currently has no auth guard and is used by the planning UI before authentication completes.

stop_ids.description: At least two stop IDs to route between

post.summary: Preview an OSRM-optimized route run without creating it
post.description: Returns the optimized route for a pool or explicit stop list. Does not persist anything. Used by the Lead planning UI to preview before committing. Note: no auth guard; intended for pre-auth planning flow.

pool_id.description: Pool ID — fetch stops from pool (option B)

ul_id.description: Azure Entra OID of the intended assignee (optional)

post.description: Creates an OSRM-optimized route run and assigns it to a UL. Requires Lead or Admin role. Writes an `assignment.create` audit log entry on success.

stop_ids.description: Explicit stop IDs (if omitted, pool stops are used)

ul_id.description: Azure Entra OID of the UL to assign

get.description: Returns the full route run including all stops and their current status. This transitional endpoint has no auth guard — prefer domain-specific accessors where possible.

post.description: Transitions the route run from planned to in_progress.

post.summary: Start a route run stop (Lead/Admin variant)
post.description: Transitions a stop from pending/planned/assigned to in_progress. Also idempotent if already in_progress. The UL variant (in routeRunStopRoutes) only allows pending → in_progress.
Marks the route run as completed/finished.

patch.summary: Assign, reassign, or unassign a route run
patch.description: Updates the `assigned_user_oid` on a route run. - If previously unassigned → writes `assignment.create` audit entry. - If reassigning → writes `assignment.reassign` audit entry. - If `assigned_user_oid` is null → writes `assignment.cancel` audit entry.

assigned_user_oid.description: Azure Entra OID of the UL to assign; null to unassign

post.summary: Skip a stop due to a safety hazard
post.description: Marks the stop as skipped, creates a hazard record, and closes the visit with outcome=skipped. Requires a safety photo to have been uploaded prior to calling this endpoint (validated server-side).

hazard_types.description: One or more safety hazard types

safety_photo_key.description: S3 key of the safety photo

safety.description: Nested form (alternative to flat fields): { hazard_types, notes, severity, safety_photo_key }

400.description: Validation error — safety photo missing or no hazard types

example.error: A safety photo is required to skip a stop

post.description: Marks the stop as done, records a clean log entry, and emits canonical observations. Requires at least one photo to have been uploaded and at least one cleaning task checked (or spotCheck=true).

duration_minutes.description: Time spent at the stop in minutes

trashVolume.description: Trash volume 0-4 (required if any cleaning task is true)

spotCheck.description: True if this was an inspection-only visit with no cleaning performed

photo_keys.description: S3 keys of completion photos (legacy — prefer uploading via /uploads/signed-url)

infraIssues.description: Infrastructure issues observed at the stop

safety.description: Optional safety observation (hazard types, severity, notes)

400.description: Validation error — missing photo, missing cleaning task, or invalid trashVolume

example.error: After photo is required to complete a stop

post.summary: Get a pre-signed S3 upload URL for a stop photo
post.description: Returns a time-limited pre-signed S3 URL for direct client-to-S3 upload. The server validates filename and MIME type before issuing the URL. The object key is server-generated (UUID-based) — the client filename is never used as the storage key. Allowed MIME types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, image/heic.

route_run_stop_id.description: ID of the route run stop this photo belongs to

filename.description: Original filename (validated, not used as storage key)

200.description: Pre-signed URL and server-generated object key

objectKey.description: Server-generated S3 object key (use this to reference the photo)

post.summary: Generate a test route run (development only)
post.description: Creates a route run for testing without authentication or OSRM optimization. **Not for production use.** This endpoint has no auth guard and is intended for local development and automated test fixtures only.

get.summary: List stops with pagination and search (admin)

parameters.name: run_date
parameters.description: Filter by run date (defaults to today)

get.description: Returns audit events in JSON (default) or CSV. Scoped to org by bearer token. Date range max 365 days; window defaults to last 30 days. Max 1000 rows per request.

200.description: Audit log entries (JSON) or CSV attachment

post.description: Recomputes the risk snapshot table from clean log history. Admin-only; may take several seconds on large datasets.

get.description: Aggregate clean events, total clean minutes, hazards reported, and high-severity hazards for today.

get.summary: Control center — active route status table
get.description: Per-route stop counts, resolved counts, emergency additions, and skip flags for planned and in-progress runs today.

200.description: Array of active route status rows

get.description: Skips by reason, total hazards, total infrastructure issues, and emergency/ad-hoc stop count for today.
Heavy stops by location, routes with high difficulty density, and hotspot area concentration. Observational intelligence — no per-worker metrics.

patch.summary: Mark or unmark a stop as a hotspot
patch.description: Updates the is_hotspot flag on the stop. UL, Lead, and Admin can toggle this.

is_hotspot.description: Whether this stop is a hotspot

patch.summary: Set the compactor flag on a stop
patch.description: Marks whether a stop has a trash compactor. Lead and Admin only.

patch.summary: Set the has-trash flag on a stop
patch.description: Marks whether a stop has a trash receptacle. Lead and Admin only.

get.description: Returns all active route pools available for assignment. Used by the Lead create-route flow.

get.summary: List assignable users (UL and Lead roles)
get.description: Returns users from the identity directory who hold UL or Lead roles. Used by the Lead assignment UI.

get.summary: Ops dashboard — aggregate metrics for today
get.description: Read-only mirror of the Admin dashboard. Returns stop count, pool count, and today's run status summary.
Returns all route pools including inactive ones. Read-only for Lead/Admin.

get.summary: List stops with pagination and search (ops read-only)
get.description: Paginated, searchable list of all stops. Read-only for Lead/Admin.

get.summary: List route runs with filters (ops read-only)
get.description: Paginated list of route runs. Read-only mirror of the Admin route runs list.

get.summary: List clean logs with filters (ops read-only)
get.description: Paginated list of clean log entries. Read-only mirror of the Admin clean logs list.

get.summary: List route overrides for a pool
get.description: Returns all per-stop overrides configured for a given route pool.

200.description: List of overrides for the pool

post.summary: Add a route override for a stop in a pool
post.description: Creates a per-stop override (e.g., exclude a stop from automated route planning for a pool).

delete.description: Removes a per-stop route override by ID.

get.summary: List asset types for a tenant
get.description: Returns all asset types configured for the specified org. Org is supplied via X-Org-Id header or ?org_id= query param.

post.summary: Create an asset type for a tenant
post.description: Registers a new asset type for the org. Used during tenant onboarding.

type_key.description: Unique slug for this asset type

get.summary: List observation types for an asset type
get.description: Returns all observation types configured for a given asset type within the org.

parameters.name: asset_type_id
parameters.description: Asset type ID to list observation types for

post.summary: Upsert observation types for an asset type
post.description: Creates or updates observation type definitions for a given asset type. Used during tenant onboarding.

post.summary: Seed asset inventory from a CSV upload
post.description: Accepts a multipart CSV file with required columns: external_id, display_name, lat, lon. Additional columns become entries in the asset attributes JSON. Used for bulk onboarding of asset inventories (e.g., importing stop data from a GIS export).

file.description: CSV file with columns external_id, display_name, lat, lon, and optional extras

asset_type_id.description: Asset type to assign to all seeded assets
